title: Vector in Java
summary: Vectors are an important data structure in Java programming that are used to store and manipulate collections of elements. A vector is similar to an array, but with some additional features that make it more flexible and convenient to use. content: Vectors are an important data structure in Java programming that are used to store and manipulate collections of elements. A vector is similar to an array, but with some additional features that make it more flexible and convenient to use. In this blog, we will explore the basics of vectors in Java, how they differ from arrays, and how to use them in your programs.
